name: STEM
description: Physics, engineering, circuits, signals вҖ” mechanics to modern engineering

topics.title: Newton's Three Laws of Motion
topics.explainer:
## Newton's Laws

### First Law (Inertia)
An object stays at rest or constant velocity unless acted upon by a net force.

### Second Law (F = ma)
Net force = mass Г— acceleration. **F = ma** is the foundation of mechanics.
- Weight: W = mg (g вүҲ 9.8 m/sВІ)
- 1 Newton = 1 kgвӢ…m/sВІ

### Third Law (Action-Reaction)
Every action has an equal and opposite reaction on a *different* object.
- You push wall вҶ’ wall pushes you
- Earth pulls you вҶ’ you pull Earth

### Free Body Diagrams
Draw ALL forces: Weight (вҶ“), Normal (вҠҘ surface), Friction (opposing motion), Applied, Tension.
topics.visual:
Free Body Diagram:
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ     Normal (N) вҶ‘   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  f_k вҶҗ в”Ңв”Җв”Ө вҶ’ F    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ         в”Ӯmв”Ӯ        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ         в””в”Җв”ҳ        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     Weight вҶ“ (mg)  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  ОЈF_x = F - f = ma в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  ОЈF_y = N - mg = 0 в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

questions.question: 5 kg block pushed with 20 N on frictionless surface. Acceleration?
If no net force acts on an object, it will:
options: Continue at constant velocity or stay at rest
questions.explanation: Newton's First Law: no net force вҶ’ no change in velocity.

questions.question: The upward force a table exerts on a book is:
options: Reaction to book's weight on Earth
questions.explanation: Normal force is the contact force perpendicular to the surface.

topics.title: Energy, Work & Power
topics.explainer:
## Energy, Work & Power

### Work
W = FвӢ…dвӢ…cos(Оё). Units: Joules (J)

### Kinetic Energy
KE = ВҪmvВІ. Doubles when speed doubles? No вҖ” **quadruples** (vВІ)!

### Potential Energy
- Gravitational: PE = mgh
- Elastic (spring): PE = ВҪkxВІ

### Conservation of Energy
KE + PE = constant (no friction)
- Ball from height h: mgh = ВҪmvВІ вҶ’ v = вҲҡ(2gh)

### Power
P = W/t = FвӢ…v. Units: Watts (W)
1 horsepower вүҲ 746 W

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ A (top) PE=max,KE=0  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в•І                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в•І    C              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    в•І  в•ұв•І              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     в•Ів•ұ  в•І             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  B       D            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ PE=0, KE=max          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ mgh_A = ВҪmvВІ_B       в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

questions.question: Ball dropped from 20m. Speed at ground? (g=10 m/sВІ)
questions.explanation: v = вҲҡ(2gh) = вҲҡ(2Г—10Г—20) = вҲҡ400 = 20 m/s.

questions.question: If a car's speed doubles, its kinetic energy:
questions.explanation: KE = ВҪmvВІ. Speed Г—2 вҶ’ KE Г—4.

topics.title: Momentum & Collisions
topics.explainer:
## Momentum

### p = mv (vector)

### Impulse: J = FО”t = О”p
Why airbags work: increase О”t вҶ’ decrease F.

### Conservation
No external forces вҶ’ ОЈp_before = ОЈp_after

### Collision Types
- **Elastic:** Momentum AND KE conserved (billiard balls)
- **Inelastic:** Only momentum conserved
- **Perfectly inelastic:** Objects stick вҶ’ v_f = (mвӮҒvвӮҒ+mвӮӮvвӮӮ)/(mвӮҒ+mвӮӮ)

### Equal mass elastic:
Velocities exchange! Moving ball stops, stationary ball moves.
topics.visual:
Elastic (equal mass):
  Before: вҶ’     (rest)
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”җ    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯm в”Ӯ v  в”Ӯm в”Ӯ 0
  в””в”Җв”Җв”ҳ    в””в”Җв”Җв”ҳ

  After: (stops)  вҶ’
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”җ    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯm в”Ӯ 0  в”Ӯm в”Ӯ v
  в””в”Җв”Җв”ҳ    в””в”Җв”Җв”ҳ
  Velocities exchange!

questions.question: 2 kg ball at 3 m/s hits stationary 2 kg ball (elastic). Result?
options: First stops, second moves at 3 m/s
questions.explanation: Equal mass elastic collision: velocities exchange.

questions.question: Why do airbags reduce injury?
questions.explanation: J = FО”t = О”p. Same О”p, larger О”t вҶ’ smaller F.

electricity-circuits.name: Electricity & Circuits
electricity-circuits.description: Ohm's law, Kirchhoff's laws, capacitors, semiconductors

topics.title: Ohm's Law & Basic Circuits
topics.explainer:
## Ohm's Law: V = IR

- V = Voltage (Volts)
- I = Current (Amps)
- R = Resistance (Ohms, О©)

### Power: P = IV = IВІR = VВІ/R

### Series
- Same current, voltages add
- R_total = RвӮҒ + RвӮӮ + RвӮғ

### Parallel
- Same voltage, currents add
- 1/R_total = 1/RвӮҒ + 1/RвӮӮ + 1/RвӮғ

### Kirchhoff's Laws
1. **KCL:** Sum of currents at node = 0
2. **KVL:** Sum of voltages around loop = 0
topics.visual:
Series:              Parallel:
  в”Ңв”Җ[R1]в”Җ[R2]в”Җ[R3]в”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җ[R1]в”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ[R2]в”Җв”Җв”Ө
  [V]               в”Ӯ  [V]     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җ[R3]в”Җв”Җв”ҳ
  R = R1+R2+R3        1/R = ОЈ1/Rбөў

questions.question: Three 6О© resistors in parallel: total resistance?
12V battery, 3A current. Power dissipated?
In a series circuit, what is constant?
questions.explanation: In series, same current flows through all components.

topics.title: Capacitors & RC Circuits
topics.explainer:
## Capacitors

### C = Q/V (Farads)
Stores energy: E = ВҪCVВІ

### Series & Parallel (opposite of resistors!)
- **Series:** 1/C_total = 1/CвӮҒ + 1/CвӮӮ
- **Parallel:** C_total = CвӮҒ + CвӮӮ

### RC Time Constant: П„ = RC
- Charging: V(t) = VвӮҖ(1 вҲ’ e^(вҲ’t/П„))
- Discharging: V(t) = VвӮҖe^(вҲ’t/П„)

### Key Points
- t = П„: ~63% charged / ~37% remaining
- t = 5П„: ~99% (practically full)

### Applications
Timing, filtering, smoothing, energy storage (camera flash).
topics.visual:
RC Charging:
  V в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
    в”Ӯ          __________
    в”Ӯ         /
    в”Ӯ        / 63% at П„
    в”Ӯ       /
    в”Ӯ      /
    в”Ӯ     /
    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ t
    0   П„   2П„  3П„  5П„

questions.question: Two 10ОјF capacitors in series: total?
questions.explanation: 1/C = 1/10+1/10 = 2/10 вҶ’ C = 5ОјF. Opposite of resistors!

questions.question: RC circuit: R=1kО©, C=1mF. Time constant?

topics.title: Semiconductors & Transistors
topics.explainer:
## Semiconductors

### Doping
- **N-type:** Extra electrons (phosphorus)
- **P-type:** Extra holes (boron)

### Diodes (PN Junction)
- Current in ONE direction (PвҶ’N)
- Forward: ~0.7V drop (Si)
- Reverse: no current (until breakdown)

### BJT Transistor
Small base current вҶ’ large collector current: I_C = ОІ Г— I_B (ОІ вүҲ 100-300)
- Switch mode: on/off
- Amplifier mode: linear

### MOSFET
- Voltage-controlled (gate voltage вҶ’ drain current)
- Very high input impedance
- Used in ALL modern digital circuits
- CMOS: NMOS + PMOS for low power
topics.visual:
NPN Transistor:
       C (collector)
       в”Ӯ
  B в”Җв”Җв”Ө  I_C = ОІ Г— I_B
       в”Ӯ
       E (emitter)

  Small I_B controls large I_C

  Diode:
  P в”Ӯ N
  вҶ’вҶ’вҶ’вҶ’вҶ’  forward bias (conducts)
  вҶҗвҶҗвҶҗвҶҗвҶҗ  reverse bias (blocks)

questions.question: NPN with ОІ=200, I_B=0.1mA. I_C = ?
Why MOSFETs over BJTs in digital circuits?
questions.explanation: MOSFET: voltage-controlled (negligible gate current), CMOS consumes power only during switching.

waves-optics.name: Waves & Optics
waves-optics.description: Wave properties, sound, light, electromagnetic spectrum

topics.title: Wave Properties
topics.explainer:
## Wave Properties

### Key Parameters
- **Wavelength (О»):** Distance between crests
- **Frequency (f):** Oscillations per second (Hz)
- **Amplitude (A):** Maximum displacement
- **Wave speed:** v = fО»

### Types
- **Transverse:** Oscillation вҠҘ travel (light, water surface)
- **Longitudinal:** Oscillation вҲҘ travel (sound)

### Wave Phenomena
- **Reflection:** Angle in = angle out
- **Refraction:** Speed change at boundary вҶ’ bending (Snell's law: nвӮҒsinОёвӮҒ = nвӮӮsinОёвӮӮ)
- **Diffraction:** Bending around obstacles
- **Interference:** Constructive (peaks align) or destructive (peak+trough)
topics.visual:
Transverse Wave:
      О»
  вҶҗв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҖвҶ’
  в•ұв•І      в•ұв•І
 в•ұ  в•І    в•ұ  в•І     A вҶ•
в•ұ    в•І  в•ұ    в•І
в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв•Ів•ұв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв•Ів•ұв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

  v = f Г— О»
  f = 1/T (period)

questions.question: A wave has f=500 Hz and О»=0.68m. Speed?
questions.explanation: v = fО» = 500 Г— 0.68 = 340 m/s (speed of sound!).

questions.question: Sound is what type of wave?
questions.explanation: Sound oscillates parallel to direction of travel (compression/rarefaction).

topics.title: Electromagnetic Spectrum
topics.explainer:
## Electromagnetic Spectrum

All EM waves travel at c = 3Г—10вҒё m/s in vacuum.

### Spectrum (low вҶ’ high frequency)
1. **Radio** (>1m): communication, WiFi
2. **Microwave** (1mm-1m): cooking, radar
3. **Infrared** (700nm-1mm): heat, remote controls
4. **Visible** (400-700nm): ROY G BIV
5. **Ultraviolet** (10-400nm): sunburn, sterilization
6. **X-ray** (0.01-10nm): medical imaging
7. **Gamma** (<0.01nm): nuclear, cancer treatment

### E = hf
Energy proportional to frequency. Higher f = more energy.
Photon energy: E = hf = hc/О» (h = 6.626Г—10вҒ»ВівҒҙ JвӢ…s)
topics.visual:
EM Spectrum:
  Radio Micro IR  Vis  UV  X  Gamma
  вҶҗв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҖвҶ’
  low f                    high f
  long О»                   short О»
  low E                    high E

  Visible: 400nm(violet)вҶ’700nm(red)

questions.question: Which EM wave has the highest energy?
questions.explanation: E = hf. Gamma has highest frequency вҶ’ highest energy.

questions.question: Speed of all EM waves in vacuum?
questions.explanation: All EM waves travel at c = 3Г—10вҒё m/s in vacuum.

topics.title: Lenses & Mirrors
topics.explainer:
## Optics

### Mirror Equation & Lens Equation
1/f = 1/d_o + 1/d_i

### Magnification
m = -d_i/d_o = h_i/h_o

### Mirrors
- **Concave:** Converging. Real images (if object > f)
- **Convex:** Diverging. Always virtual, upright, smaller.

### Lenses
- **Convex (converging):** Real images possible
- **Concave (diverging):** Always virtual

### Sign Conventions
- Real image: d_i positive
- Virtual image: d_i negative
- Upright image: m positive
- Inverted: m negative

### Total Internal Reflection
When angle > critical angle at interface (dense вҶ’ less dense). Used in fiber optics.
topics.visual:
Convex Lens:
       F    F
  в”Җв”Җв”ҖвҶ’ в”Ӯ\  /в”Ӯ в”Җв”Җв”ҖвҶ’
  в”Җв”Җв”ҖвҶ’ в”Ӯ \/ в”Ӯ в”Җв”Җв”ҖвҶ’  converge
  в”Җв”Җв”ҖвҶ’ в”Ӯ /\ в”Ӯ в”Җв”Җв”ҖвҶ’  at focal
  в”Җв”Җв”ҖвҶ’ в”Ӯ/  \в”Ӯ в”Җв”Җв”ҖвҶ’  point

  1/f = 1/d_o + 1/d_i

questions.question: Object at 30cm from lens with f=20cm. Image distance?
Convex mirrors always produce images that are:
questions.explanation: Convex (diverging) mirrors always produce virtual, upright, diminished images.

topics.title: Heat Transfer & Specific Heat
topics.explainer:
## Heat Transfer

### Q = mcО”T
- Q = heat energy (Joules)
- m = mass
- c = specific heat capacity
- О”T = temperature change

### Specific Heat
Amount of energy to raise 1 kg by 1В°C.
- Water: 4186 J/(kgвӢ…В°C) вҖ” very high!
- Iron: 450 J/(kgвӢ…В°C)

### Transfer Methods
- **Conduction:** Through material (touching). Metals are good conductors.
- **Convection:** Through fluid motion. Hot air rises.
- **Radiation:** Through EM waves. No medium needed (Sun вҶ’ Earth).

### Phase Changes
At phase change, temperature stays constant!
- Q = mL (L = latent heat)
- Fusion (melting), Vaporization (boiling)
topics.visual:
Heating Curve:
  T в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
    в”Ӯ         _____ gas
    в”Ӯ        /
    в”Ӯ  _____/ boiling
    в”Ӯ / liquid
    в”Ӯ/ melting
    в”Ӯ___ solid
    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ Q
    Flat parts = phase change

questions.question: Energy to heat 2 kg water by 10В°C? (c=4186 J/kgВ°C)
During a phase change, temperature:
questions.explanation: Energy goes into breaking bonds, not raising temperature.

topics.title: Laws of Thermodynamics
topics.explainer:
## Laws of Thermodynamics

### Zeroth Law
If A is in thermal equilibrium with B, and B with C, then A with C.
This defines temperature!

### First Law (Energy Conservation)
О”U = Q вҲ’ W
- О”U = change in internal energy
- Q = heat added
- W = work done BY system

### Second Law
Entropy of isolated system never decreases.
- Heat flows hot вҶ’ cold spontaneously
- No heat engine is 100% efficient
- Carnot efficiency: О· = 1 вҲ’ T_cold/T_hot

### Third Law
Entropy вҶ’ 0 as T вҶ’ 0 K (absolute zero). Can never reach 0 K.
topics.visual:
Heat Engine:
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ Hot (T_H) в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
       в”Ӯ Q_H
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  Engine  в”ӮвҶ’ W = Q_H - Q_C
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
       в”Ӯ Q_C
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”ӮCold (T_C)в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
  О· = W/Q_H = 1 - T_C/T_H

questions.question: Carnot efficiency with T_H=600K, T_C=300K?
The Second Law implies:
options: No heat engine can be 100% efficient
Heat flows cold to hot naturally
questions.explanation: Second Law: some energy always wasted as heat вҶ’ no 100% efficiency.

topics.title: Ideal Gas Law
topics.explainer:
## Ideal Gas Law

### PV = nRT
- P = pressure (Pa)
- V = volume (mВі)
- n = moles
- R = 8.314 J/(molвӢ…K)
- T = temperature (Kelvin!)

### Special Cases
- **Boyle's:** PвӮҒVвӮҒ = PвӮӮVвӮӮ (constant T)
- **Charles's:** VвӮҒ/TвӮҒ = VвӮӮ/TвӮӮ (constant P)
- **Gay-Lussac's:** PвӮҒ/TвӮҒ = PвӮӮ/TвӮӮ (constant V)

### Kinetic Theory
KE_avg = (3/2)kT where k = 1.38Г—10вҒ»ВІВі J/K
- Temperature is average kinetic energy
- Higher T вҶ’ faster molecules

### STP
Standard: 0В°C (273.15 K), 1 atm (101325 Pa)
1 mol of ideal gas at STP = 22.4 L
topics.visual:
PV = nRT
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ Boyle's (const T):      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  PвҶ‘ вҶ’ VвҶ“  (squeeze)     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ Charles's (const P):    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  TвҶ‘ вҶ’ VвҶ‘  (heatвҶ’expand) в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ Gay-Lussac's (const V): в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  TвҶ‘ вҶ’ PвҶ‘  (heatвҶ’press)  в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

questions.question: Gas at 2 atm and 3 L is compressed to 1 L (const T). New pressure?
questions.explanation: Boyle's: PвӮҒVвӮҒ=PвӮӮVвӮӮ вҶ’ 2Г—3 = PвӮӮГ—1 вҶ’ PвӮӮ = 6 atm.

questions.question: Temperature in ideal gas law must be in:
questions.explanation: PV=nRT requires absolute temperature (Kelvin). K = В°C + 273.15.

topics.title: Fourier Transform Basics
topics.explainer:
## Fourier Transform

### Core Idea
Any periodic signal = sum of sinusoids at different frequencies.

### Time Domain вҶ’ Frequency Domain
Fourier transform decomposes a signal into its frequency components.
- F(Пү) = вҲ« f(t) e^(-jПүt) dt

### Key Properties
- Linearity: F{af+bg} = aF{f}+bF{g}
- Time shift вҶ’ phase change
- Convolution in time вҶ’ multiplication in frequency

### DFT (Discrete)
For digital signals: N samples вҶ’ N frequency bins.
FFT computes DFT in O(N log N) instead of O(NВІ).

### Applications
- Audio: spectrum analysis, EQ, compression (MP3)
- Images: JPEG compression
- Communications: OFDM (WiFi, 4G/5G)
- Signal processing: filtering, noise removal
topics.visual:
Time вҶ’ Frequency:
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  FFT  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ вҲҝвҲҝвҲҝвҲҝвҲҝвҲҝ   в”Ӯ в”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҖвҶ’ в”Ӯ  |  |    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ complex  в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ  |  |  | в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ waveform в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ  |  |  | в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ  |  |  | в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ       в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
   time domain      frequency domain

  Signal = sum of pure frequencies

questions.question: FFT computes DFT in what time complexity?
questions.explanation: FFT reduces DFT from O(NВІ) to O(N log N) using divide-and-conquer.

questions.question: Convolution in time domain equals what in frequency domain?
questions.explanation: Convolution theorem: f*g in time = FВ·G in frequency. This makes filtering efficient.

topics.title: Digital Signal Processing
topics.explainer:
## Digital Signal Processing

### Sampling
Convert analog вҶ’ digital by measuring at regular intervals.

### Nyquist Theorem
Sampling rate must be вүҘ 2Г— highest frequency to avoid aliasing.
- Audio: 20kHz max вҶ’ sample at 44.1kHz (CD quality)
- If you undersample вҶ’ aliasing (high freq appears as low)

### Quantization
Round samples to discrete levels.
- 8-bit: 256 levels
- 16-bit: 65536 levels (CD audio)
- 24-bit: 16.7M levels (studio)

### Digital Filters
- **Low-pass:** Remove high frequencies (smoothing)
- **High-pass:** Remove low frequencies (edge detection)
- **Band-pass:** Keep only a frequency range
- **FIR:** Finite Impulse Response (always stable)
- **IIR:** Infinite Impulse Response (can be unstable)

questions.question: Minimum sampling rate to capture a 10kHz signal?
questions.explanation: Nyquist: f_sample вүҘ 2 Г— f_max = 2 Г— 10kHz = 20kHz.

questions.question: A low-pass filter:
questions.explanation: Low-pass allows frequencies below cutoff, removes higher ones (smoothing).
